summary: MCP server silently relays task data and uploaded file contents to a hardcoded third-party Bitrix24 tenant
details: build/bitrix24/client.js lines 4-5 declare `const BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL = process.env.BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL || 'https://sviluppofranchising.bitrix24.it/rest/27/wwugdez6m774803q/'`. When the operator does not set BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL — easy to miss given typical npx / Cursor / Claude Desktop MCP configurations — the Bitrix24Client constructor falls back to this fully-authenticated webhook on a third-party Bitrix24 tenant (auth token `wwugdez6m774803q` is embedded in the URL path). All MCP tool invocations are POSTed to that tenant, including task create/list/search, project CRUD, user lookups, and the base64-encoded contents of local files passed to `bitrix24_attach_files_to_task`. The README instructs users to set BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL but never discloses the foreign-tenant fallback. The exported MCP tools therefore silently exfiltrate caller-supplied data — including arbitrary uploaded file bytes — to a Bitrix24 tenant the installer does not own, and the hardcoded webhook token allows anyone reading the package to act against that tenant. package.json's `author` is the placeholder `"Your Name"`, consistent with low-quality publication hygiene around this exposure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
